node.statement: (4.1): ||q_{r,s}||_omega <= C sqrt(eta) ||r|| ||s|| for r,s in A=Im P (def-near-fixed-algebra), every state seminorm omega (node 1.1). PROOF. The map (a,b)->q_{a,b}:=P(a‚àòb)-a‚àòb is symmetric (‚àò symmetric) and real-bilinear on A (P real-linear, ‚àò bilinear; A=Im P a real subspace, GT-Pprops); write q_t:=q_{t,t}=P(t^2)-t^2 for the diagonal (def-square-hole). Polarisation identity: for a,b in A, a‚àòb=(1/4)((a+b)^2-(a-b)^2), so by bilinearity q_{a,b}=(1/4)(q_{a+b}-q_{a-b}). For any lambda>0, bilinearity gives q_{r,s}=q_{lambda r, s/lambda}, hence q_{r,s}=(1/4)(q_{lambda r+(s/lambda)}-q_{lambda r-(s/lambda)}). By the seminorm triangle inequality (node 1.1) and the DIAGONAL square-hole bound ||q_t||_omega<=C sqrt(eta)||t||^2 for t in A (GT-square-hole; note lambda r ¬± s/lambda in A since A is a real subspace): ||q_{r,s}||_omega <= (1/4)(||q_{lambda r+s/lambda}||_omega+||q_{lambda r-s/lambda}||_omega) <= (1/4)C sqrt(eta)(||lambda r+s/lambda||^2+||lambda r-s/lambda||^2). By the parallelogram-type bound ||p+w||^2+||p-w||^2<=2||p||^2+2||w||^2+... actually use ||p¬±w||^2<=(||p||+||w||)^2 so sum<=2(||p||+||w||)^2; cleaner: ||lambda r ¬± s/lambda||^2 <= 2 lambda^2||r||^2 + 2 lambda^{-2}||s||^2 (since ||x+y||^2<=2||x||^2+2||y||^2 in the order-unit norm, triangle ineq + (p+q)^2<=2p^2+2q^2). Summing the two: ||lambda r+s/lambda||^2+||lambda r-s/lambda||^2 <= 4 lambda^2||r||^2+4 lambda^{-2}||s||^2. Thus ||q_{r,s}||_omega <= C sqrt(eta)(lambda^2||r||^2+lambda^{-2}||s||^2). If r=0 or s=0 the claim is trivial (q_{r,s}=0). Otherwise choose lambda^2=||s||/||r||, giving lambda^2||r||^2+lambda^{-2}||s||^2=2||r||||s||, hence ||q_{r,s}||_omega<=2C sqrt(eta)||r||||s||=C' sqrt(eta)||r||||s|| with C'=2C universal dimension-free. Uses node 1.1, GT-square-hole, GT-Pprops, def-near-fixed-algebra, def-square-hole.
